summary: Matrix recording of the second set captures a fire‑filled, improvisational night – Help>Slip>Franklin’s, scorching Dark Star and more – with generally good sound quality despite a few audience‑only complaints.
review: The tape (a matrix source covering only the second set) delivers one of the most energetic shows in the collection: the Help → Slip → Franklin’s opening, blistering Scarlet Begonias/Fire jams, a trippy Dark Star and a rousing Werewolves of London finale.  Reviewers rave about the flow and improvisational power, while noting that the mix sounds more like an audience recording to some listeners but is still listenable overall.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews (9 out of 11) are 5‑star and praise the musical performance; only a few mention the recording sounding like an audience tape, so the show quality drives a high rating with good but not perfect sound.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most listeners find the matrix source clear enough to enjoy the set, though one reviewer says it sounds like a pure audience recording and another flags it as not a true board mix.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second‑set launch with Help>Slip>Franklin’s sets a high bar, followed by a string of tight improvisational jams that keep the energy rolling to a climactic Werewolves of London finish.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh is praised for ‘dropping bombs’ on his bass throughout the set.
